info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A physician is ordering a thick blood film for a patient with suspected lymphatic filariasis caused by *Wuchereria bancrofti*. At what time should the blood specimen be collected to maximize diagnostic sensitivity?

options.label: During the onset of a high fever
Early morning immediately after waking
Mid-day when the patient is most active
Late afternoon during the peak of symptoms
At night due to the periodicity of the microfilariae

q2.prompt: A patient with lymphatic filariasis is administered diethylcarbamazine (DEC). Shortly after the first dose, the patient develops a systemic reaction characterized by fever, malaise, and muscle pain. Which of the following best explains the pathophysiology of this clinical response?

options.label: Accidental activation of host glutamate-gated chloride channels
Direct toxic effect of the drug on the vascular endothelium
Host inflammatory response to protein release from dying parasites
Immediate type I hypersensitivity to the chemical structure of DEC
Rapid accumulation of the drug within the central nervous system

q3.prompt: A public health official is investigating a food-borne outbreak of *Cyclospora cayetanensis* linked to imported raspberries. Why is direct person-to-person transmission via fresh fecal matter not observed with this parasite?

options.label: Oocysts are only produced during the sexual cycle in feline hosts
Oocysts must mature and sporulate in the environment to become infectious
Parasites require an intermediate snail host for larval development
The infectious stage is a fragile trophozoite that dies outside the host
Trophozoites must encyst within the gallbladder before being passed

q4.prompt: A 34-year-old male returns from a tropical region and presents with periodic fever paroxysms. A Giemsa-stained thin blood smear is performed, revealing enlarged, pale-staining infected erythrocytes that are distinctly oval in shape with characteristic fimbriated (jagged) edges. Schüffner's dots are clearly visible within the host cell cytoplasm. Which of the following *Plasmodium* species is the most likely causative agent?

options.label: *Plasmodium falciparum*
*Plasmodium knowlesi*
*Plasmodium malariae*
*Plasmodium ovale*
*Plasmodium vivax*

q5.prompt: A patient is treated for malaria, but the infection returns several weeks later. The physician determines the recurrence is a "relapse" rather than a "recrudescence." Which of the following mechanisms differentiates a relapse in *Plasmodium vivax* from a recrudescence in *Plasmodium falciparum*?

options.label: Activation of dormant hypnozoites in the liver
Infection of new erythrocytes by surviving gametocytes
Persistence of a low-level erythrocytic parasitemia
Infection of new erythrocytes by surviving merozoites
Re-entry of the parasite from the splenic reservoir

q6.prompt: A patient with a chronic, latent Toxoplasma gondii infection is at risk for reactivation if they become immunocompromised. Which of the following stages of the parasite is responsible for maintaining this lifelong infection within host tissue cysts?

options.label: Amastigote
Bradyzoite
Epimastigote
Tachyzoite
Oocyst

q7.prompt: A public health initiative aims to disrupt the life cycle of *Opisthorchis viverrini* in an endemic region. Which of the following organisms serves as the primary first intermediate host that must be targeted?

options.label: *Bithynia* snail
*Cyclops* copepod
Cyprinid fish
Freshwater crab
Pig

q8.prompt: Which developmental stages of Giardia lamblia (also known as G. duodenalis) represent the infectious form and the diagnostic form found in feces, respectively?

options.label: Trophozoite and mature cyst
Mature cyst and trophozoite or cyst
Immature cyst and trophozoite
Sporulated oocyst and trophozoite
Trophozoite and embryonated egg

q9.prompt: A patient presents with severe weight loss, muscle wasting, and persistent, massive watery diarrhea. Stool examination reveals peanut-shaped eggs with striated shells and flattened mucoid plugs at both poles. The patient most likely acquired this infection from consuming which of the following?

options.label: Raw freshwater fish
Undercooked poultry
Contaminated soil on vegetables
Raw buffalo meat
Undercooked pork

q10.prompt: A public health researcher is investigating the transmission of lymphatic filariasis in a region where both *Wuchereria bancrofti* and *Brugia malayi* are endemic. Which of the following features is unique to *Wuchereria bancrofti* and distinguishes it from *Brugia malayi*?

options.label: Possession of a sheath in the microfilaria stage
Requirement of a mosquito intermediate host
Absence of a known animal reservoir host
Presence of two distinct terminal nuclei in the tail
Clinical presentation of elephantiasis in the lower limbs
